confidence_rationale: This video compilation contains institutional tours, expert interviews, real operative footage (clinical surgery, not FLS training), robotic surgical demonstrations, academic lectures, and educational platform content. There is NO FLS task performance (Peg Transfer, Pattern Cut, Endoloop, Extracorporeal Suture, or Intracorporeal Suture) visible in any frame. The video is purely educational/instructional content about minimally invasive surgery programs and techniques, not a trainee performance evaluation.

technique_summary: This video is an educational documentary/promotional piece about Indiana University's minimally invasive surgery fellowship program, featuring expert faculty (Dr. Chandru Sundaram, Dr. Jason Farrow) discussing clinical techniques, program structure, and academic training. It includes real operative footage, interviews, institutional information, and online educational platform demonstrations. It does NOT contain any FLS (Fundamentals of Laparoscopic Surgery) task assessment material.

demonstrated_techniques: Clinical robotic-assisted surgical techniques (da Vinci system)
verbal_cues: Content appears to discuss minimally invasive surgery fellowship training
Expert commentary on surgical education and techniques

assessment_notes: UNUSABLE FOR FLS SCORING. This is institutional/educational promotional content, not an FLS task performance video. To score an FLS task, provide a video showing a trainee performing one of the five MISTELS tasks within the standardized FLS trainer box with clear task start/end timestamps.
